claims.text: The variant NM_000152.5:c.1935C>A (p.Asp645Glu) is listed in the expanded variant nomenclature footnote of Table 2 with ClinVar ID VCV000004029.

In the Mini-COMET Cohort 1 overall population (n=6), at least two patients carried c.1935C>A always in cis with c.1726G>A (p.Gly576Ser) on one allele, in compound heterozygous configuration with other variants (c.[784G>A];[1935C>A;1726G>A] and c.[1062C>G;1286A>G];[1935C>A;1726G>A]).

In the Mini-COMET Cohort 2 overall population (n=5), at least two patients carried c.1935C>A always in cis with c.1726G>A on at least one allele, including one patient homozygous for [1935C>A;1726G>A] and one compound heterozygous with [2842INST].

All patients in the Mini-COMET trial carrying c.1935C>A had infantile-onset Pompe disease (IOPD) and had previously received alglucosidase alfa with clinical decline or suboptimal response before switching to avalglucosidase alfa.

citations.quote: Mini-COMET is a Phase 2, multi-stage, randomized, open-label, multicenter, multinational, ascending-dose cohort trial primarily evaluating the safety of avalglucosidase alfa in patients with IOPD who previously treated with a stable dose of alglucosidase alfa. Patients who showed clinical decline with alglucosidase alfa treatment classified as stage 1 (Cohort 1 and 2), and patients who showed suboptimal clinical response with alglucosidase alfa treatment classified as stage 2 (Cohort 3)
